entities.name: JMT Network Services Public Company Limited

financial.note: BBL provides significant corporate credit lines for NPL portfolio acquisitions; rising interest rates increase the cost of carry for distressed assets.
Collaborates on the JK AMC venture; KBANK's willingness to offload NPLs at favorable prices directly supports JMT's inorganic growth.
Provides inter-group liquidity and strategic capital support; JMT's performance is the primary profit engine for the JMART ecosystem.

supply_chain.note: 50/50 JV with KBANK; serves as a dedicated pipeline for distressed assets, significantly reducing acquisition search costs.
Banks sell NPL portfolios to JMT to clean their balance sheets; a higher banking sector NPL ratio increases JMT's inventory.
Rising legal processing fees or appraisal costs for secured assets increase the 'Cost of Collection' and compress operating margins.

competitors.note: Direct rival for bank NPL tenders; intense bidding competition for high-quality portfolios can lead to yield compression.
Competes in the unsecured consumer debt segment; market share shifts impact JMT's dominance in small-ticket collection.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Higher rates increase borrowing costs for acquisitions and reduce the disposable income of debtors, slowing 'Cash Collection' velocity.
High systemic debt levels expand the total addressable market (TAM) for NPL acquisition but increase Expected Credit Loss (ECL) risks.
Directly correlates with the ability of consumer debtors to settle accounts; rising unemployment reduces JMT's collection efficiency.
Impacts the recovery value of secured NPLs and Foreclosed Assets (NPA); higher prices enable higher auction disposal gains.
